title: IR schema rule: duplicate slot names within an aggregate or event are rejected

evidence.ref: Backlog item #12 from D027 — 4 work-item negative fixtures could not test what their filenames implied because the IR validator had no rule for duplicate-slot-name and 3 fixture data files were structurally wrong relative to their names; data also had fake 47-char sha that masked the intended error
027-work-item-fixture-meta-correctness — D027 re-typed the meta but left the data fixes for a follow-up; D029 IS that follow-up
007-agent-roles-glob-fix-and-ir-schema-and-ir-fixture-types — introduced the IR schema validator; D029 fills a missing rule from that decision's scope
fixtures/negative/work-item/work-item-invalid-duplicate-slot-name.json — first fixture to actually exercise the new duplicate-slot rule

guards.ref: internal/probe.validateIRDoc — seenSlotNames map in the aggregate/event branch; reports first collision as 'slots[i].name <name>: duplicate of slots[j].name (slot names must be unique within an aggregate)'
concept-map C-014 — codifies the rule; examples + counterexamples; guard_candidate pointer to validateIRDoc

examples: work-item-invalid-duplicate-slot-name.json had two slots both named 'id'; with sha fixed to a real 64-hex value the validator now reports the duplicate-slot error and the fixture stops being a lie.
work-item-invalid-missing-required.json — sha fixed; source.dsl_file removed; validator reports 'source.dsl_file "": must match dsl/*.lisp' (an honest missing-required-field test on source.dsl_file).
work-item-invalid-missing-slots.json — sha fixed; slots changed to []; validator reports 'kind=aggregate requires at least one slot' (the existing D007 rule).

work-item-invalid-slot-name-null.json — sha fixed; data has slots[1].name = null which JSON unmarshals to empty string; validator reports 'slots[1].name "": must be kebab-case' (the existing rule, now actually reached).
counterexamples: Triple-duplicate slots[]: 3 slots all named 'id' — validator reports only the FIRST collision (slots[1] vs slots[0]); not a bug, the data is rejected once that's enough to fail.
kind=query unchanged: D029's duplicate-slot rule lives in the aggregate/event branch only; query has no slots.
